name: Anita Baker
abstract: Anita Denise Baker (born January 26, 1958) is an American jazz and soul singer. She is known for her soulful ballads, particularly from the height of the quiet storm period in the 1980s.

concerts.label: A Tribute to the Music of Motown
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Houston Person Part 1
about_quotes.evidence: they were playing, The Manhattans, and Anita Baker, and all these people
about_quotes.doc_title: Houston Person Part 1 interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Shaughnessy Part II
about_quotes.evidence: And after we get done she comes up to me and she says “you heard the record.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Ed Shaughnessy Part II interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 25, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Ruthie Foster
about_quotes.evidence: I got a chance to not work with but talk with Barry Eastman, who was the producer for Anita Baker.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ruthie Foster interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 28, 2023
